entries.definition:
tyranny, rebellion (17:60) وَنُخَوِّفُهُمْ فَمَا يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا طُغْيَانًا كَبِيرًا We warn them, but it only increases them in great rebellion 2 obstinacy (6:110) وَنُقَلِّبُ أَفْئِدَتَهُمْ وَأَبْصَارَهُمْ كَمَا لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِهِ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَنَذَرُهُمْ فِي طُغْيَانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ We will make their hearts and their eyes waver, just as they did not believe in it the first time, and leave them to flounder in their obstinacy.
طَاغُوت ṭāghūt [n.] 1 [generic for] false deity/deities (2:256) فَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِالطَّاغُوتِ وَيُؤْمِنْ بِاللَّهِ فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقَىٰ so whoever rejects false gods and believes in God has taken grasp of the firmest link 2 [generic for] evil powers; variously named by the interpreters as: the Devil, diviners, enchanters, any head or leader in error, the idol ʾal-Lāt (q.v.) or Kaʿb ibn ʾal-ʾashraf, a Jewish man who directed hostilities against the new religion (4:60) يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يَتَحَاكَمُوا إِلَى الطَّاغُوتِ وَقَدْ أُمِرُوا أَنْ يَكْفُرُوا بِهِ they desire to seek the arbitration of false idols (or, leaders of disbelievers) when they have been ordered to reject them?
entries.definition_english:
tyranny, rebellion (17:60) وَنُخَوِّفُهُمْ فَمَا يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا طُغْيَانًا كَبِيرًا We warn them, but it only increases them in great rebellion 2 obstinacy (6:110) وَنُقَلِّبُ أَفْئِدَتَهُمْ وَأَبْصَارَهُمْ كَمَا لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِهِ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَنَذَرُهُمْ فِي طُغْيَانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ We will make their hearts and their eyes waver, just as they did not believe in it the first time, and leave them to flounder in their obstinacy.
طَاغُوت ṭāghūt [n.] 1 [generic for] false deity/deities (2:256) فَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِالطَّاغُوتِ وَيُؤْمِنْ بِاللَّهِ فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقَىٰ so whoever rejects false gods and believes in God has taken grasp of the firmest link 2 [generic for] evil powers; variously named by the interpreters as: the Devil, diviners, enchanters, any head or leader in error, the idol ʾal-Lāt (q.v.) or Kaʿb ibn ʾal-ʾashraf, a Jewish man who directed hostilities against the new religion (4:60) يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يَتَحَاكَمُوا إِلَى الطَّاغُوتِ وَقَدْ أُمِرُوا أَنْ يَكْفُرُوا بِهِ they desire to seek the arbitration of false idols (or, leaders of disbelievers) when they have been ordered to reject them?

entries.definition:
ṭ-f-f a small measure, trivial matter; to be deficient; to be miserly; to become near, to become due. Of this root, المطففين ʾal-muṭaffifīn occurs once in the Qurʾan.
مُطَفِّفِين muṭaffifīn [pl. of act. part. مُطَفِّف muṭaffif] one who does not give full measure, one who gives less than is due, swindler (83:1–3) وَيْلٌ لِلْمُطَفِّفِينَ الَّذِينَ إِذَا اكْتَالُوا عَلَى النَّاسِ يَسْتَوْفُونَ وَإِذَا كَالُوهُمْ أَوْ وَزَنُوهُمْ يُخْسِرُونَ woe to those who give short measure, who demand of other people full measure for themselves, but give less than they should when it is they who weigh or measure for others!; * المطففين name of Sura 83, Meccan sura, so-named because of the reference in verse 1 to ‘those who give short measure’.

568
entries.definition_english:
ṭ-f-f a small measure, trivial matter; to be deficient; to be miserly; to become near, to become due. Of this root, المطففين ʾal-muṭaffifīn occurs once in the Qurʾan.

568
